brand.colors: Logo mark: linear gradient #48BBF1 (vivid sky cyan) to #FFF9C7 (pale warm gold) â€” gold is logo-artwork only, never a UI token. Site token system (DESIGN.md 'White Catalog'): --accent-media #3AAED8 (brand cyan as light, NON-TEXT, media/logo/glow only), --accent #15718F (deep cyan, focus rings + inline links + interactive text), --text/ink #121417 (with dark-system ancestor #0C0D10 from the rejected June dark redesign â€” the natural surface family for a dark studio app), --surface #FAFAF8 paper, --surface-raised #F4F4F2, --text-muted #5C6167, --error #A8231B, hairlines rgba(18,20,23,0.14), --radius 0 (all-sharp). For the dark studio tool: invert to ink-family surfaces (#0C0D10 / #121417 / #1A1D21 ladder), keep the same two-value cyan accent system, no pure #000 or #FFF.
brand.voice: Restrained label copy: short declaratives, momentum framing, sentence case everywhere, functional verbs for microcopy (Notify Me, Subscribe, Send, Contact â€” one CTA string per intent). Never decorative, never enthusiastic. Hard-banned vocabulary in any visible string: innovate, disrupt, transform, cutting-edge, elevate, seamless, unleash, next-gen, revolutionize; deficit framing (underserved, deserve, overlooked, forgotten, rescue, revive); 'world-first', 'the worlds we love', 'visual studies', 'experiences', 'playable'; any real IP/franchise name; internal codenames (Signal Reel, No.001, Meta-Drop, Track/Move vocabulary). Zero em-dashes and en-dashes anywhere (mechanically grep-scanned before merge). All visible strings live in a typed copy module, never inline in components.
brand.positioning: Vixio Creatives is a creative label for story-rich worlds â€” public framing: 'worlds worth entering.' It ships a slate of films and objects, presented catalog-first: the work is the argument, the label never describes itself (self-description confined to /studio, footer descriptor, metadata). Status vocabulary is industry-plain (In Production, In Development, Coming {year}). Founder Denis operates in an EP seat; AI filmmaking is the production craft behind the slate (the 'AI Filmmaking' library: Cinematic Filmmaking, Story System, Virtual Camera, Style Guide â€” vocabulary of shots, cameras, style, story systems). Firewall test for any outward string: would it sting if a courted rights holder read it? Layer 1 (founder) and Layer 2 (Compass/strategy/TIFFCOM roadmap) content never reaches public surfaces.
brand.existingSiteStack: vixio-creatives-website: Next.js 16 App Router + React 19 + TypeScript 5.7 (strict: true) + Tailwind v4 via @tailwindcss/postcss + framer-motion 12 + @phosphor-icons/react + Supabase + Resend; Playwright for e2e; fonts via next/font (Space Grotesk 700 display, tracking -0.02em; Manrope 400/500 body, line-height 1.6, max 65ch). Conventions: design tokens as CSS custom properties in app/globals.css consumed via var(--token); all visible strings in lib/copy.ts (typed SiteCopy interface), all content data in lib/slate.ts (typed SlateEntry, data-only additions); components never define strings (ADR-14); path alias @/*; ADRs in docs/adr (0001-0014) + CONTEXT.md glossary govern decisions; motion inventory is a closed list gated on useReducedMotion (whileInView fade-rise 24px/0.6s/ease [0.16,1,0.3,1] once, hover scale 1.02 inside overflow-hidden, mount fade) â€” anything beyond needs a new ADR; radius 0, zero eyebrows site-wide, light theme locked on the marketing site (the dark cinematic system was explicitly rejected THERE for chrome-over-work reasons â€” a studio tool is a different surface where dark is correct, but the same doctrine applies: the media is the hero, chrome recedes). Pre-flight: npm run typecheck && lint && build, copy ban-scan, dash-scan.

tsStyleRules: strict: true tsconfig is non-negotiable (the existing repo already has it); add noUncheckedIndexedAccess for new projects; never weaken with as any â€” fix the type at the source.

Model state with discriminated unions, never boolean flags: type GenJob = { status: 'queued' } | { status: 'running'; progress: number } | { status: 'done'; output: AssetRef } | { status: 'failed'; error: JobError }. Switch on the discriminant and let TypeScript prove exhaustiveness (the repo already does this: sectionHeading has 'no fallback: the union is total, TypeScript proves it').

No enums. Use string-literal unions for closed sets (export type SlateStatus = 'in-production' | 'in-development' | 'coming' | 'released' â€” the existing pattern) and as const objects when you need runtime values: const PANELS = ['timeline', 'shots', 'style'] as const; type Panel = typeof PANELS[number].

type for unions, mapped types, and function types; interface acceptable for plain object shapes (the repo uses interface SlateEntry + type unions â€” keep that split consistent rather than relitigating it per file).
Use satisfies to validate config/data against a type without widening: export const slate = [...] satisfies SlateEntry[] â€” keeps literal inference while proving shape.

Branded types for IDs that must not cross-contaminate: type ShotId = string & { readonly __brand: 'ShotId' }; type AssetId = string & { readonly __brand: 'AssetId' } â€” a studio app juggles shots, takes, assets, jobs; raw strings will eventually swap.

Result-style error handling at seams: async operations return { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: E } discriminated unions instead of throwing across module boundaries; throwing is reserved for programmer errors. UI maps the error variant to an inline state, never a blank screen.
Named exports only; no default exports except where Next.js requires them (page.tsx, layout.tsx, route.ts).

Small, deep modules: a module earns its existence by hiding complexity behind a small interface (the 'interface' = everything a caller must know: types, invariants, error modes, ordering â€” not just the signature). Apply the deletion test: if deleting the module makes complexity vanish, it was a pass-through; if complexity reappears across N callers, it was earning its keep.

Design it twice: for any non-trivial module interface (the generation queue, the timeline model, the asset store), sketch 2-3 radically different shapes (minimal 1-3 methods vs flexible vs common-case-optimized) before committing. Evaluate on interface simplicity, depth (small interface hiding significant complexity = good; large interface, thin implementation = bad), and ease-of-misuse.

One adapter = hypothetical seam, two adapters = real seam: don't pre-build provider abstractions for image/video gen APIs until a second provider actually exists; when it does, the seam is the interface the first caller already needed.

Data-only content changes: follow the ADR-05 pattern â€” adding a shot, a style preset, or a slate entry is an edit to a typed data module (lib/slate.ts style), never a component edit. Display resolution logic (labels, ordering, status composition) lives next to the data, fully typed, with pure functions (statusLabel/statusLine/sectionHeading are the house pattern).

All visible strings in one typed copy module (lib/copy.ts, interface SiteCopy with readonly tuples where arity matters: facts: readonly [string, string, string, string]); components never define strings (ADR-14). This is also the enforcement point for the copy ban-scan.

TDD as vertical slices: one behavior test -> minimal implementation -> next test. Never write all tests up front (horizontal slicing tests imagined behavior and couples to shape). Tests exercise public interfaces only and must survive internal refactors; if renaming an internal function breaks a test, the test was wrong. Never refactor while red.

Prototype before committing on uncertain designs: throwaway code answering one question (state-model question -> tiny runnable harness; look-and-feel question -> several radically different UI variants on one route behind a URL search param). Clearly marked, one command to run, no persistence, surface full state after every action, delete or absorb when answered â€” capture only the verdict (ADR/commit message).

Maintain CONTEXT.md as a pure domain glossary (shot, take, asset, slate, treatment, status â€” zero implementation details) and use those exact terms in type names, test names, and modules; don't drift between 'clip'/'shot'/'segment'. Write an ADR only when a decision is hard to reverse AND surprising without context AND the result of a real trade-off; the existing repo's docs/adr 0001-0014 is the precedent.
Zoom-out discipline: when entering unfamiliar code, first produce a map of modules and callers in glossary vocabulary before editing; respect existing ADRs in the area or explicitly reopen them.
designRules: [ADAPTED from design-taste-frontend, which scopes itself to landing pages and explicitly excludes dense product UI â€” its token, contrast, motion, AI-tell, and state rules carry over; its hero/section/marketing rules are dropped or remapped as noted.] Dials for this app: DESIGN_VARIANCE 3 (panels are a calm grid; variance lives inside the media), MOTION_INTENSITY 3 (feedback-only), VISUAL_DENSITY 7-8 (cockpit: tight padding, 1px hairlines separate data instead of card boxes, font-mono for all numbers).

Theme lock: ONE theme, dark, app-wide. Surfaces ladder from the ink family (#0C0D10 base canvas, #121417 panels, ~#1A1D21 raised/hover), never pure #000000 or #FFFFFF; text off-white (e.g. #E8EAED) with a muted tier ~ #9BA1A6. No section/panel inverts to light mid-app.

One accent system, locked: brand cyan in two values â€” #3AAED8 as light (active states, selection, progress, media glow, logo) and a text-safe cyan for links/focus (on dark, #3AAED8 itself passes for large/interactive text; verify 4.5:1 for body-size). No second accent ever appears; no AI purple, no neon gradients, no mesh blobs. Semantic colors limited to error (#A8231B family lightened for dark) and a job-status green if truly needed.

Shape consistency lock: pick ONE radius system and document it. House precedent is radius 0 (all-sharp, 'White Catalog'); for a tool, either keep all-sharp or one documented small radius (4-6px) for every interactive element â€” never mixed pills + sharp panels.

Typography: Space Grotesk 700, tracking -0.02em for panel titles, view names, and brand moments (sentence case â€” zero uppercase-tracked eyebrows anywhere, the house rule); Manrope 400/500 for UI text and body (line-height 1.5-1.6); a mono (Geist Mono or JetBrains Mono) for timecodes, frame counts, seeds, dimensions, generation params â€” VISUAL_DENSITY > 7 makes mono numerals mandatory. Load via next/font. Never Inter-by-default.

Layout: CSS Grid for the workspace shell (grid-template-columns with fr units; e.g. rail / browser / canvas / inspector), never flex percentage math. Panel chrome is hairlines (1px rgba(255,255,255,0.08-0.12)) and spacing, not drop shadows; when a shadow is needed (popovers, drag ghosts) tint it toward the surface hue, never pure black at high opacity. Cards only where elevation means something (floating panels, modals); group everything else with border-t / divide-y / negative space.

Full interactive state cycles for every surface (the #1 LLM omission): loading = skeletons matching the final layout's exact shape and aspect ratio (never generic spinners â€” a generating shot shows a shimmering 16:9 placeholder at its real size); empty states are composed and tell the user how to populate ('No shots yet. Add one from the script panel.'); errors are inline and contextual (toasts only for transient); :active gets scale-[0.98] or -translate-y-[1px] tactile feedback.

Forms and inspectors: label above input always, no placeholder-as-label ever, helper text present in markup, error text below input, gap-2 input blocks; every input, placeholder, focus ring, and error passes WCAG AA against its panel background; visible 2px accent focus rings offset 2px; 44px minimum touch/click targets; keyboard shortcuts rendered as <kbd> keys (1px hairline border, 4px radius, mono font) â€” the minimalist-ui keystroke pattern fits an editor perfectly.

Motion must be motivated: every animation justifiable in one sentence as feedback, state transition, or hierarchy â€” nothing decorative, no infinite loops EXCEPT genuine live status (a rendering/generating indicator is real semantic state, the one place a pulse is allowed). Use Motion (motion/react, framer-motion alias already in the repo): useMotionValue/useTransform for anything continuous (scrubbing, drag, playhead) â€” never useState for continuous values; window.addEventListener('scroll') is a hard ban; animate transform and opacity only; custom cubic-bezier (ease [0.16,1,0.3,1] is the house curve), never linear or ease-in-out; everything gates on useReducedMotion; useEffect animations have strict cleanups; isolate motion in 'use client' leaf components.

Performance: backdrop-blur only on fixed/sticky overlays (command palette, modals), never on scrolling containers; grain/noise only on fixed pointer-events-none layers (and probably skip it in a tool); will-change sparingly; documented z-index scale in a constants file (e.g. base 0, sticky chrome 10, dropdowns 20, modals 30, toasts 40) â€” no arbitrary z-50 spam; virtualize long shot/asset lists; min-h-[100dvh] never h-screen.

Icons: @phosphor-icons/react only (already the house dependency), one family, one standardized weight/strokeWidth across the app; never hand-roll SVG icon paths; no emojis anywhere in UI text or markup.

Copy: sentence case everywhere; functional verbs (Generate, Render, Export, Save â€” one label per intent app-wide, no 'Export' here and 'Download' there); zero em/en dashes (mechanical grep scan); no hype vocabulary (Section 6 ban list); all strings in the typed copy module; copy self-audit before ship â€” re-read every visible string for broken grammar or LLM-cute phrasing and replace with plain functional sentences.

AI tells banned: no neon/outer glows by default (inner 1px borders and tinted shadows instead â€” the single sanctioned glow is brand cyan as light inside media frames, the --accent-media doctrine); no gradient text; no custom cursors; no decorative status dots (a dot means real live state, max sparingly); no fake-precise numbers in mock data; no div-built fake screenshots â€” previews show real media or designed placeholder treatments (paper fields, ink geometry, hairline glyphs, brand cyan as light â€” the PLACEHOLDER-ASSETS.md doctrine), never gray boxes.

[ADAPTED] Landing-page-only rules consciously dropped: hero stack discipline, section-layout-repetition ban, zigzag cap, bento cell counts, logo walls, testimonial rules, scroll-hijack/GSAP skeletons, marquee cap â€” replaced by the panel/density rules above. Rules adapted rather than dropped: 'eyebrow restraint' hardened to the house 'zero eyebrows'; 'hero needs a real visual' becomes 'the canvas/viewer is the hero'; 'one design system per project' becomes Tailwind v4 + CSS custom-property tokens + owned components (no Material/shadcn graft); 'dark mode protocol' becomes single dark theme with hierarchy parity and brand fidelity.

Accessibility floor: WCAG 2.1 AA on every text/background pair in the dark theme; skip link; semantic landmarks; aria-describedby on errors with live regions; decorative treatments aria-hidden; reduced-motion path for every animation; test keyboard-only operation of the editor's primary flow before calling any feature done.
imagegenUiRules: The media is the hero; chrome recedes. (Direct transfer of the Vixio doctrine 'the catalog is the argument' + imagegen IMAGE_USAGE_PRIORITY 9.) The canvas/viewer/filmstrip gets the space and the contrast; panels, toolbars, and inspectors are quiet near-monochrome surfaces that never compete with generated frames. Never let UI decoration (gradients, glows, patterns) sit next to user imagery â€” the user's frames must be the most saturated thing on screen.

One palette, one image treatment, locked app-wide (Multi-Image Consistency Rule adapted): thumbnails, comparison views, and galleries render media untinted and unfiltered at true aspect ratio â€” no duotone, overlay, or crop the user didn't choose. UI-applied overlays exist only to make text readable over imagery (dark tonal scrim, never a color wash) and only where text genuinely must sit on the image.

Anti-AI-slop in the tool itself: no purple/blue AI-glow defaults, no floating blobs/orbs, no glowing edges, no gradient-text 'premium' shortcuts, no fake dashboards or vanity KPI strips (Data-viz restraint: charts only where the data is real â€” queue depth, render time; proof is the frames themselves). Brand cyan as light is the only sanctioned glow, confined to media frames and live-generation states.

Generation states are designed, not generic: a queued/running job shows a skeleton or designed treatment at the OUTPUT's exact aspect ratio and grid slot (16:9 shot stays 16:9 while generating) with shimmering placeholder, mono progress text, and the cyan live indicator; failures show the prompt/params that failed inline with a retry action â€” never an empty gray box, never a centered spinner replacing the layout.

Galleries and filmstrips follow the gapless/rhythm discipline: mathematically clean grids (Pristine Gapless Bento logic â€” exactly as many cells as items, no orphan placeholder tiles), mixed sizes only with a documented rule (featured take large, alternates small); square/16:9 masonry must feel curated, not scrapbook-random; image-dense regions get tight gaps, control regions get air (cross-section contrast applied inside one screen).

Typography over imagery: text on frames sits in a clean safe area with a contrast scrim and passes AA; captions live below media, outside the frame, one line, functional ('Take 3, seed 48291' in mono) â€” never decorative pills/tags overlaid on the user's images (the overlaid-pill ban transfers directly: no 'Shot Â· 02' badges stamped on frames).

One unmistakable primary action per viewport tier (CTA specificity rule): the Generate/Render action reads as THE action; secondary actions (duplicate, variations, export) are visibly secondary â€” ghost/outline/icon-only â€” never visual clones of the primary. Vary affordance by job, not by whim: pill â‰  ghost â‰  inline-underline used consistently for the same intent everywhere.

Implementation-clarity bias (IMPLEMENTATION_CLARITY 9 transferred): every screen should read like a buildable comp â€” obvious hierarchy, obvious reading order, strong but few display moments (panel titles in Space Grotesk), supporting text brief; if a screen needs explanation, the layout failed.

Reference-image workflow for new screens (the imagegen skill's actual job, used at design time): when concepting a new view, generate one horizontal reference image per distinct screen/panel state in the locked palette (Deep Dark Mode paradigm: charcoal/graphite/ink, elegant glow only when justified), at 16:9/16:10, varied composition anchors, then implement against it â€” never design dense editor screens by free-association in code.

High-end finish details that transfer from high-end-visual-design: hairline borders (white/10) instead of generic gray 1px solid; the double-bezel nested-frame treatment reserved for the one place it means something â€” the media viewport (outer shell bg-white/5 + ring-1 ring-white/10 + p-1.5, inner core with concentric radius) so frames read as physical slides in a tray; ultra-diffuse low-opacity shadows only; custom cubic-bezier on every transition; staggered (60-80ms) reveals when a batch of generated thumbnails lands â€” motion as 'your results arrived', not spectacle.
preflightChecklist: npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run build all pass (offline: NEXT_FONT_GOOGLE_MOCKED_RESPONSES=$(pwd)/font-mocks.js).
ZERO em-dashes or en-dashes anywhere visible: LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 grep -rnP "[\x{2013}\x{2014}]" app components lib returns zero hits.

Copy ban-scan over the typed copy module returns zero hits (hype verbs, deficit framing, internal codenames, real IP names); every visible string re-read for broken grammar or LLM-cute phrasing (copy self-audit).
Theme lock: one dark theme app-wide, no panel or view flips to light; no pure #000000 or #FFFFFF anywhere.
Color consistency lock: exactly one accent system (brand cyan, two values) used identically across every panel; no stray second accent, no AI purple, no neon gradients.
Shape consistency lock: one documented corner-radius system applied to every container, button, and input.
Button contrast: every CTA's text passes WCAG AA (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large) against its background; no ghost button illegible over media without a scrim.
No CTA label wraps to 2+ lines at desktop; no two CTAs with the same intent under different labels (one verb per intent app-wide).
Form contrast: inputs, placeholders, focus rings, labels, helper and error text all pass AA against panel backgrounds; labels above inputs; no placeholder-as-label.
Empty, loading, and error states implemented for every data surface; generation jobs show shape-matched skeletons at true aspect ratio, never generic spinners or gray boxes.
Motion motivated: every animation justifiable in one sentence (feedback / state transition / hierarchy); complete motion inventory documented; anything beyond it needs an ADR.
Reduced motion: every animation gated on useReducedMotion / prefers-reduced-motion with a static path; useEffect animations have strict cleanup functions.
No window.addEventListener('scroll') anywhere; continuous values driven by useMotionValue/useTransform, never useState; animations touch only transform and opacity.
backdrop-blur only on fixed/sticky overlays; grain/noise (if any) only on fixed pointer-events-none layers; z-index values come from the documented scale constants file only.
min-h-[100dvh] never h-screen; explicit responsive collapse declared for every multi-column panel layout (what happens below 1024px and 768px is written in the component, not assumed).
Icons exclusively @phosphor-icons/react, one weight standardized; zero hand-rolled SVG icon paths; zero emojis in code, markup, or visible text.
Zero uppercase-tracked eyebrow labels; sentence case everywhere; all numbers/timecodes/seeds in mono.
No AI tells: no decorative status dots (live-state only), no fake-precise mock numbers, no div-built fake screenshots, no pills/tags overlaid on user media, no version stamps in chrome.
All visible strings live in the typed copy module and all content in typed data modules; components define zero strings; adding content is a data-only change.

Accessibility pass: skip link, semantic landmarks, 2px accent focus rings (offset 2px), 44px targets, aria-describedby + live regions on errors, decorative treatments aria-hidden, keyboard-only walkthrough of the primary editor flow completed.
Core Web Vitals plausible (LCP < 2.5s with priority media, INP < 200ms with heavy work off main thread, CLS < 0.1 with reserved space for media and fonts); long lists virtualized.
One design system per project: Tailwind v4 + CSS custom-property tokens + owned components; no second component library grafted in.
